unicode.value:
2920/101
DIPLOMA IN INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY
INTRODUCTION TO INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY AND ETHICS

March/April 2023

Time: 3 hours

Instructions to Candidates:

  • This paper consists of EIGHT questions.
  • Answer any FIVE questions in the answer booklet provided.
  • Candidates should answer the questions in English.

Question 1

(a) Outline four advantages of outsourcing acquisition method. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Cost reduction through economies of scale
  • Access to specialized expertise and technologies
  • Allows focus on core business functions
  • Reduced infrastructure investment needs

(b) Explain two characteristics of the third-generation computers. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Used integrated circuits (ICs) which made them smaller and more reliable than previous generations
  • Introduced keyboard and monitor as standard input/output devices

(c) A computer laboratory has been secured using facial recognition. Explain three limitations of this system. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Can be affected by changes in lighting conditions, leading to false rejections
  • May have difficulty recognizing faces with masks, glasses, or other facial coverings
  • System performance can degrade over time as facial features change

(d) (i) With the aid of a diagram, describe peer to peer computer network. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

A peer-to-peer network is a decentralized network where each computer acts as both client and server, sharing resources directly without a central server.

(d) (ii) Outline two characteristics of the network in d(i). (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • No central server required - all computers have equal status
  • Direct resource sharing between computers

Question 2

(a) Outline four benefits of self-awareness to a college student. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Better understanding of personal strengths and weaknesses
  • Improved emotional intelligence and relationships
  • Enhanced decision-making abilities
  • Better stress management and coping skills

(b) Shelyn, a Module II student, prefers using an impact printer. Explain three reasons that could have led to this preference. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Ability to create multiple copies simultaneously using carbon paper
  • Lower cost of operation compared to non-impact printers
  • Durability and reliability in harsh environments

(c) Distinguish between intra-group and inter-group conflict. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Intra-group conflict occurs between members within the same group, while inter-group conflict occurs between different groups within an organization.

(d) Explain three circumstances that could make an organisation fail in its corporate social responsibility. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Insufficient financial resources to support CSR initiatives
  • Lack of commitment from top management
  • Poor communication and implementation of CSR policies

Question 3

(a) Explain two benefits of copyright. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Protects creators' rights and ensures they receive compensation for their work
  • Encourages innovation and creativity by providing legal protection for intellectual property

(b) An organisation intends to develop its ICT legislation. Explain three sources of these legislation. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Government regulations and national ICT policies
  • International standards and best practices
  • Industry-specific regulatory requirements

(c) Differentiate between data bus and address bus as used in computers. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Data bus carries actual data between CPU and other components, while address bus carries memory addresses to specify locations for reading or writing data.

(d) Heztech Technical institute has launched a virtual learning programme for its students.

(i) Explain two challenges of this programme to the students. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Limited access to reliable internet connectivity
  • Lack of direct face-to-face interaction with instructors

(ii) Outline two opportunities that this programme could create. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Flexible learning schedule for working students
  • Access to education for geographically distant students

Question 4

(a) Outline four advantages of a light pen as an input device. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Direct and intuitive pointing device
  • High precision for drawing and selecting
  • Natural hand-eye coordination
  • Good for graphics and design work

(b) Dan suffers from low self-esteem. Explain three symptoms that he could be exhibiting. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Difficulty accepting compliments and positive feedback
  • Self-critical thoughts and negative self-talk
  • Avoiding social interactions and new challenges

(c) Distinguish between twisted pair and fibre optic cables as used in networking. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Twisted pair cables use copper wires and transmit electrical signals, while fiber optic cables use glass/plastic fibers and transmit light signals. Fiber optic offers higher bandwidth and longer transmission distances.

(d) Mark uses pirated software in his laptop. Explain three reasons that could have led to this use. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • High cost of original software licenses
  • Easy accessibility of pirated versions
  • Lack of awareness about legal implications

Question 5

(a) (i) Define repetitive strain injury as used in ergonomics. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

Repetitive strain injury is a condition caused by prolonged repetitive movements, often resulting in pain and damage to muscles, tendons, and nerves.

(ii) Outline two symptoms exhibited by a computer user suffering from repetitive strain injury. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Pain and tingling in wrists and fingers
  • Numbness and weakness in affected areas

(b) Explain three benefits of time management to a student. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Improved academic performance through better study planning
  • Reduced stress and anxiety levels
  • Better work-life balance

(c) Differentiate between ink jet and laser printers. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Ink jet printers spray tiny droplets of liquid ink onto paper, while laser printers use toner powder and heat to fuse the image onto paper. Laser printers are typically faster and more cost-effective for high-volume printing.

(d) Employees at Geztech Company have work related conflicts among themselves. Explain three possible causes of these conflicts. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Poor communication between team members
  • Unclear roles and responsibilities
  • Competition for limited resources

Question 6

(a) Explain two features of system software. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Manages hardware resources and provides platform for application software
  • Performs basic system operations like file management and memory allocation

(b) Distinguish between withdrawing and smoothing conflict resolution techniques. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Withdrawing involves removing oneself from the conflict situation, while smoothing involves emphasizing areas of agreement and downplaying disagreements to maintain harmony.

(c) Reztech Company intends to acquire a mainframe computer. Explain three benefits the company will accrue from this computer. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • High processing power for large-scale operations
  • Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously
  • Enhanced data security and reliability

(d) An organisation has a responsibility of protecting the confidentiality of its data. Explain three safeguarding measures that could be put in place to achieve this. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Implementation of strong encryption protocols
  • Regular security audits and updates
  • Access control and user authentication systems

Question 7

(a) Outline three uses of computers in an engineering firm. (3 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Computer-Aided Design (CAD) for drafting and modeling
  • Project management and scheduling
  • Structural analysis and simulation

(b) Explain each of the following terms as used in ICT:

(i) compiler;

Sample Answer:

A program that translates source code written in a high-level programming language into machine code.

(ii) defragmenter;

Sample Answer:

A utility program that reorganizes fragmented files on a disk to improve system performance.

(iii) disk partition. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

A logical division of a hard disk drive that can be managed separately from other segments.

(c) Distinguish between laptop and tablet computers. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Laptops have a physical keyboard and clamshell design, running full desktop operating systems, while tablets are touchscreen devices with virtual keyboards, typically running mobile operating systems.

(d) Figure 2 shows a computer storage device. Use it to answer the questions that follow.

(i) Identify this device. (1 mark)

Sample Answer:

Hard Disk Drive (HDD)

(ii) Explain three characteristics of the device identified in (i) (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Non-volatile storage that retains data when powered off
  • Uses magnetic platters for data storage
  • Higher storage capacity compared to solid-state drives

Question 8

(a) Outline four duties of a network administrator. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Managing network security and access controls
  • Monitoring network performance and troubleshooting issues
  • Installing and configuring network hardware and software
  • Managing user accounts and access permissions

(b) Explain three circumstances that necessitates information privacy in an organisation. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Protection of sensitive customer data and personal information
  • Safeguarding proprietary business information and trade secrets
  • Compliance with data protection regulations and laws

(c) The following are computer keyboard keys:

Home, Tab, Enter, End, Insert, Ctrl, Alt, Page Up.

Classify each of these keys as either navigation or special. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Navigation keys: Home, Tab, End, Page Up

Special keys: Enter, Insert, Ctrl, Alt

(d) An organisation uses an in-house software. Explain three disadvantages of this software. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • High development and maintenance costs
  • Time-consuming development process
  • Limited support and documentation compared to commercial software

© 2023 The Kenya National Examinations Council

INTRODUCTION TO INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY AND ETHICS

November 2022

THE KENYA NATIONAL EXAMINATIONS COUNCIL

DIPLOMA IN INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY

MODULE I

INTRODUCTION TO INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY AND ETHICS

2920/101

Time: 3 hours

INSTRUCTIONS TO CANDIDATES

This paper consists of EIGHT questions.

Answer any FIVE of the EIGHT questions in the answer booklet provided.

Candidates should answer the questions in English.

This paper consists of 3 printed pages.

Candidates should check the question paper to ascertain that all the pages are printed as indicated and that no questions are missing. © 2022 The Kenya National Examinations Council.

Question 1

(a) Outline four uses of computers in the mining industry. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Geological Data Analysis: Analyzing geological data to identify potential mining sites.
  2. Mine Planning and Design: Simulating and designing mine layouts for optimal extraction.
  3. Equipment Monitoring and Control: Monitoring the performance and controlling heavy machinery used in mining.
  4. Safety and Environmental Monitoring: Tracking environmental conditions and ensuring safety protocols.
(b) Explain three characteristics of mini computers. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Mid-Range Processing Power: Mini computers offer more processing power than microcomputers but less than mainframes, suitable for small to medium-sized organizations.
  2. Multi-User Support: They can support multiple users simultaneously, often used as servers in a network.
  3. Larger Size and Cost: Physically larger and more expensive than microcomputers, requiring a dedicated space.
(c) Elimu Institute intends to acquire computers by leasing. Explain three challenges the institute is likely to encounter. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Higher Long-Term Cost: Leasing may be more expensive in the long run compared to purchasing.
  2. Dependence on Lessor: Reliance on the leasing company for maintenance and support, which may cause delays.
  3. Limited Customization: Restrictions on modifying or upgrading the leased computers.
(d) With the aid of a diagram, describe mesh computer network topology. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

In a mesh topology, each device is connected to every other device in the network. This creates redundant paths for data transmission. If one link fails, data can be rerouted through other connections.

Image shows nodes interconnected with each other

Question 2

(a) Outline four advantages of a wireless computer mouse. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Increased Mobility: Freedom to move the mouse without being restricted by a cable.
  2. Reduced Clutter: No cable to tangle or clutter the workspace.
  3. Ergonomics: Can be used in a more comfortable position, reducing strain.
  4. Convenience: Easy to connect and use with various devices.
(b) A student has been experiencing stress due to low academic grades. Explain three ways the student could manage this stress. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Time Management: Create a study schedule and prioritize tasks to improve academic performance.
  2. Seek Help: Talk to a teacher, counselor, or tutor for academic support and guidance.
  3. Practice Relaxation Techniques: Engage in activities like exercise, meditation, or deep breathing to reduce stress levels.
(c) Differentiate between copyright and patent as used in information communication technology legislation. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Copyright: Protects the expression of an idea (e.g., software code, website content). It prevents unauthorized copying, distribution, and modification.

Patent: Protects inventions (e.g., a new algorithm, a hardware design). It grants the inventor exclusive rights to use, sell, and manufacture the invention for a certain period.

(d) Ziptech Company disposed their old computers in an open field. Explain three hazards this disposal could have on the environment. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Soil Contamination: Heavy metals (lead, mercury) and other toxic substances from the computers can leach into the soil.
  2. Water Pollution: Rainwater can carry these contaminants into nearby water sources.
  3. Air Pollution: Burning of e-waste releases harmful chemicals into the air.

Question 3

(a) Outline four causes of conflicts among employees in an organisation. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Communication Breakdown: Lack of clear communication or misinterpretations.
  2. Personality Clashes: Differences in personalities and work styles.
  3. Resource Competition: Competition for limited resources (budget, equipment, promotions).
  4. Unclear Roles and Responsibilities: Ambiguity about job duties and responsibilities.
(b) A computer technician preferred to use a coaxial cable in a computer network. Explain three reasons for this preference. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. High Bandwidth: Coaxial cables can transmit large amounts of data at high speeds.
  2. Resistance to Interference: They are shielded, making them less susceptible to electromagnetic interference.
  3. Durability: Coaxial cables are relatively robust and can withstand some physical stress.
(c) Anita an ICT student has been experiencing low self-esteem. Explain three possible causes that could have led to this. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Academic Difficulties: Struggling with coursework or exams can lead to feelings of inadequacy.
  2. Social Isolation: Lack of social connections or feeling excluded from peer groups.
  3. Cyberbullying: Experiencing harassment or bullying online.
(d) Figure 1 shows a computer port. Use it to answer the questions that follow. (1+1+2 Marks)

Figure 1

(i) Identify this port. (1 mark)

Sample Answer: USB Port (likely USB Type-A based on the image).

(ii) State two devices that could be connected through the port identified in (i). (1 mark)

Sample Answer:

  1. Keyboard
  2. Mouse

(iii) Outline two advantages of the port identified in (i) (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Universal Compatibility: Supports a wide range of devices.
  2. Plug and Play: Devices are automatically detected and configured when connected.

Question 4

(a) Outline four factors a company would consider before engaging in corporate social responsibility. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Cost Implications: The financial resources required to implement and sustain CSR initiatives.
  2. Stakeholder Expectations: The expectations of customers, employees, investors, and the community regarding the company's social and environmental impact.
  3. Alignment with Business Goals: How CSR activities align with the company's overall mission and objectives.
  4. Potential Benefits: The positive outcomes of CSR, such as improved brand reputation and customer loyalty.
(b) Differentiate between collaborative and accommodative conflict management approaches. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Collaborative: A win-win approach where both parties work together to find a solution that satisfies everyone's needs and concerns.

Accommodative: One party prioritizes the needs and concerns of the other party, often yielding their own position to maintain harmony.

(c) Explain three circumstances that could necessitate the use of a light pen as a computer input device. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Graphic Design and CAD: Precise drawing and editing on screen, especially in applications like Computer-Aided Design.
  2. Interactive Presentations: Directly highlighting or annotating content on a projected screen during presentations.
  3. Form Filling on Touch Screens: Selecting options or writing directly on touch-sensitive screens for data entry.
(d) Goldy Company hired a system designer. Explain three roles that this computer personnel would play in the company (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Analyzing User Requirements: Understanding the needs of users and translating them into technical specifications for new systems.
  2. Designing System Architecture: Creating the overall structure and components of the system, including hardware and software.
  3. Developing Prototypes: Building preliminary versions of the system to test and refine the design.

Question 5

(a) Outline four disadvantages of a magnetic tape as a computer storage device. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Slow Access Time: Data is accessed sequentially, making it slow to retrieve specific files.
  2. Data Degradation: Tapes can degrade over time, leading to data loss.
  3. Sensitivity to Environment: Tapes are sensitive to temperature, humidity, and magnetic fields.
  4. Specialized Hardware: Requires a tape drive, which is not a standard component in modern computers.
(b) Describe three features of second generation computers. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Use of Transistors: Replaced vacuum tubes, making computers smaller, faster, and more reliable.
  2. Magnetic Core Memory: Used magnetic cores for main memory, improving speed and capacity.
  3. High-Level Programming Languages: Development of languages like FORTRAN and COBOL made programming easier.
(c) Paul has been selling pirated software. Explain three reasons that could have led to this unethical behaviour. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Financial Gain: Selling pirated software can be profitable due to low production costs.
  2. Lack of Awareness: Ignorance of copyright laws and the consequences of software piracy.
  3. Accessibility: Easy access to pirated software through the internet or informal markets.
(d) Differentiate between defragmenter and compressor utilities software. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Defragmenter: Reorganizes fragmented files on a hard drive to improve access speed and overall system performance.

Compressor: Reduces the size of files to save storage space and facilitate faster transfer over networks.

Question 6

(a) Outline four circumstances that could lead to the use of outsourcing method for software acquisition. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Lack of In-House Expertise: When the organization lacks the necessary skills or resources to develop the software internally.
  2. Cost Reduction: Outsourcing to a company in a region with lower labor costs can be more economical.
  3. Focus on Core Competencies: Allows the organization to focus on its primary business activities.
  4. Faster Development Time: Outsourcing can speed up the development process due to the availability of specialized resources.
(b) Describe three characteristics of optical storage devices. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Data Storage Using Lasers: Data is written and read using laser technology.
  2. Portability: Optical discs (CDs, DVDs, Blu-ray) are portable and easy to transport.
  3. Relatively Low Cost Per GB: Optical media is generally inexpensive compared to other storage options.
(c) Joyce, a programme uses an ergonomic seat in her office. Explain three benefits of using this seat. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Improved Posture: Supports the natural curvature of the spine, reducing back pain.
  2. Increased Comfort: Provides better cushioning and support, reducing discomfort during long work hours.
  3. Reduced Risk of Musculoskeletal Disorders: Helps prevent conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome and back injuries.
(d) Differentiate between install and configure as used in computer software. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Install: The process of placing the software files onto the computer's storage system (e.g., hard drive) so it can be run.

Configure: The process of customizing the software settings after installation to meet specific user preferences or system requirements.

Question 7

(a) Outline four negative impacts of information communication technology to a college student. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Distraction: Social media and online entertainment can divert attention from studies.
  2. Cyberbullying: Online harassment and bullying can negatively impact mental health.
  3. Information Overload: Difficulty in managing and processing the vast amount of information available online.
  4. Reduced Physical Activity: Excessive use of technology can lead to a sedentary lifestyle.
(b) Differentiate between of-the-shelf and bespoke software. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Off-the-shelf: Pre-built software designed for a general audience with standard features and functionalities.

Bespoke: Custom-made software developed specifically for a particular organization or user, tailored to their unique requirements.

(c) Freetech Company has implemented user authentication measures for data security. Explain three challenges the company is likely to encounter. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. User Resistance: Employees may find the authentication process inconvenient or time-consuming.
  2. System Complexity: Managing and maintaining complex authentication systems can be challenging.
  3. Cost of Implementation: Implementing and maintaining strong authentication measures can be expensive.
(d) Helen, an ICT student possesses good interpersonal skills. Explain three such skills. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Communication: Effectively conveying information and ideas, both verbally and non-verbally.
  2. Active Listening: Paying attention to and understanding what others are saying.
  3. Empathy: Understanding and sharing the feelings of others.

Question 8

(a) Explain three advantages of a non-impact printer. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Quiet Operation: Non-impact printers (like inkjet and laser printers) are much quieter than impact printers.
  2. High Print Quality: They produce higher-quality images and text, especially for graphics.
  3. Faster Print Speed: Generally faster than impact printers, particularly for complex documents.
(b) Differentiate between intranet and extranet as used in computer networks. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Intranet: A private network accessible only to members of an organization (e.g., employees). It's used for internal communication and resource sharing.

Extranet: A private network that extends access to authorized external users (e.g., suppliers, customers) in addition to internal users. It allows controlled collaboration and information sharing with external parties.

(c) The following are examples of systems controls: employee rotation, password, CCTV, locks, biometric and motivation. Classify each of these examples to its respective type of control. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Employee Rotation: Preventive Control (specifically, administrative)
  • Password: Preventive Control (specifically, logical/technical)
  • CCTV: Detective Control (specifically, physical)
  • Locks: Preventive Control (specifically, physical)
  • Biometric: Preventive Control (specifically, logical/technical)
  • Motivation: Preventive Control (specifically, administrative)
(d) Gaztech Company has put in place measures to reduce unethical behaviour in its information communication technology department. Explain two such measures. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Code of Ethics: Establishing a clear code of conduct outlining expected ethical behavior for all IT personnel.
  2. Regular Training: Providing training on ethics, security awareness, and compliance with relevant regulations.
THIS IS THE LAST PRINTED PAGE.

2920/101
THE KENYA NATIONAL EXAMINATIONS COUNCIL

DIPLOMA IN INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY

INTRODUCTION TO INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY AND ETHICS
MODULE I
Time: 3 hours
July 2022

INSTRUCTIONS TO CANDIDATES

This paper consists of EIGHT questions.

Answer any FIVE of the EIGHT questions in the answer booklet provided.

Candidates should answer the questions in English.

1.

(a) Outline four benefits of time management skills to a student. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Improved academic performance: Effective time management helps students allocate sufficient time for studying, leading to better understanding and grades.
  2. Reduced stress and anxiety: Planning and managing time effectively reduces the feeling of being overwhelmed, thus lowering stress levels.
  3. Better work-life balance: Time management allows students to balance academic work with social life, hobbies, and personal commitments, promoting overall well-being.
  4. Enhanced organizational skills: Developing time management skills enhances a student's ability to organize tasks, prioritize activities, and meet deadlines, valuable skills for future careers.

(b) Describe three characteristics of data bus in a computer central processing unit (CPU). (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Width: The data bus width determines the number of bits that can be transferred simultaneously. A wider data bus (e.g., 64-bit) allows for faster data transfer compared to a narrower one (e.g., 32-bit), improving processing speed.
  2. Speed: Data buses operate at a specific clock speed, measured in Hertz (Hz). Higher clock speeds mean faster data transfer rates, contributing to quicker communication between the CPU and other components.
  3. Direction: Data buses can be unidirectional or bidirectional. A bidirectional data bus allows data to flow in both directions between the CPU and memory or peripherals, enabling efficient communication for both reading and writing data.

(c) Distinguish between a switch and a bridge computer network device. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Both switches and bridges are used to connect network segments, but they differ in functionality:

  • Bridge: Operates at the Data Link Layer (Layer 2) of the OSI model. It forwards data packets based on MAC addresses. Bridges typically have fewer ports and are used to connect two network segments, reducing collision domains. They learn MAC addresses and filter traffic, but are less sophisticated than switches.
  • Switch: Also operates at the Data Link Layer (Layer 2) but is more advanced than a bridge. Switches have multiple ports and can connect many network devices. They learn MAC addresses and forward traffic only to the port where the destination MAC address is located, significantly reducing collisions and improving network efficiency. Switches offer higher port density and faster performance compared to bridges.

(d) A supervisor uses reactive techniques to manage workplace conflicts. Explain three likely outcomes of this technique. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

Reactive conflict management involves addressing conflicts after they have arisen. Three likely outcomes of using reactive techniques are:

  1. Escalation of conflict: By delaying intervention, minor conflicts can escalate into more significant issues. Employees may feel ignored or that their concerns are not valued, leading to increased frustration and animosity.
  2. Decreased productivity and morale: Unresolved conflicts can disrupt workflow, reduce team collaboration, and lower employee morale. Employees may become disengaged, and the work environment can become tense and unproductive.
  3. Damage to relationships: Reactive approaches often deal with symptoms rather than root causes. This can lead to superficial resolutions, and underlying issues remain, damaging interpersonal relationships and trust among team members over time.

2.

(a) Outline four challenges of online learning to a student. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Technical issues: Reliable internet access and functional devices are essential for online learning. Students in areas with poor connectivity or without adequate technology may face significant barriers.
  2. Lack of face-to-face interaction: Online learning can reduce direct interaction with instructors and peers, potentially leading to feelings of isolation and hindering collaborative learning experiences.
  3. Self-discipline and motivation: Online learning requires a high degree of self-discipline and motivation as students need to manage their study time and stay engaged without constant external supervision.
  4. Digital literacy skills: Students need to be proficient in using various digital tools and platforms for online learning. Lack of digital literacy can be a significant challenge, especially for students less familiar with technology.

(b) Explain two positive impacts of ICT to a manufacturing company. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Increased efficiency and productivity: ICT tools like automation systems, Computer-Aided Design (CAD), and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software streamline processes, reduce errors, and optimize resource utilization, leading to increased production efficiency and output.
  2. Improved communication and collaboration: ICT enables seamless communication and data sharing across different departments and locations within a manufacturing company, as well as with suppliers and customers. This enhances collaboration, speeds up decision-making, and improves supply chain management.

(c) Janet, an ICT student, suffers from depression. Describe three symptoms that she may exhibit. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Persistent sadness or low mood: Janet may experience a prolonged feeling of sadness, emptiness, or hopelessness that lasts for most of the day, nearly every day. This is a core symptom of depression and can significantly impact her daily life.
  2. Loss of interest or pleasure in activities: Janet might lose interest in activities she once enjoyed, such as hobbies, social events, or even studying ICT. This lack of pleasure, known as anhedonia, is a significant indicator of depression.
  3. Changes in sleep and appetite: Depression can disrupt sleep patterns, leading to insomnia (difficulty sleeping) or hypersomnia (excessive sleeping). It can also affect appetite, causing significant weight loss or gain due to decreased or increased appetite.

(d) An organisation uses avoid-avoid conflict resolution strategy. Explain three problems associated with this strategy. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

Avoid-avoid conflict resolution involves ignoring or sidestepping conflict situations rather than addressing them directly. Three problems associated with this strategy are:

  1. Unresolved issues fester and worsen: Avoiding conflict does not make it disappear. Underlying issues remain unaddressed and can escalate over time, leading to more significant and complex problems later on.
  2. Reduced trust and communication: When conflicts are avoided, it can create a climate of mistrust and poor communication. Employees may feel their concerns are not valued, leading to decreased openness and honesty in interactions.
  3. Missed opportunities for growth and improvement: Conflict, when managed constructively, can be an opportunity for learning and improvement. Avoidance prevents the organisation from identifying root causes of problems, making necessary changes, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement.

3.

(a) Outline two roles of a database administrator in an organisation. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Database design and implementation: DBAs are responsible for designing the database structure, including tables, relationships, and constraints, to meet the organization's data storage and retrieval needs. They also oversee the implementation of the database system.
  2. Performance monitoring and tuning: DBAs monitor database performance, identify bottlenecks, and implement tuning strategies to ensure optimal database speed, efficiency, and reliability. This includes optimizing queries, managing indexes, and adjusting database configurations.

(b) Bora institute is in the process of acquiring computer software. Describe three methods that they could use. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Purchasing off-the-shelf software: Bora Institute can buy commercially available software packages that meet their general needs. This is often the quickest and most cost-effective option for common software like office suites or accounting software.
  2. Developing custom software: If off-the-shelf software doesn't meet specific requirements, Bora Institute can commission the development of custom software tailored to their exact needs. This provides a solution that is precisely aligned with their processes but can be more expensive and time-consuming.
  3. Using open-source software: Bora Institute can utilize open-source software, which is typically free to use and modify. This option can be cost-effective and offer flexibility, but may require in-house expertise for implementation and support.

(c) A musician decided to use a DVD-RW to store his music. Explain three reasons that could have led to this decision. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Large storage capacity: DVD-RWs offer a significant storage capacity (typically 4.7GB or 8.5GB for dual-layer), which is sufficient to store a substantial amount of music files, especially in compressed formats like MP3. This makes it suitable for archiving entire albums or collections.
  2. Rewritability: DVD-RW discs are rewritable, meaning the musician can erase and rewrite data multiple times. This is advantageous for managing and updating music collections, allowing for flexibility in adding, removing, or modifying music files on the disc.
  3. Portability and compatibility: DVD-RW discs are portable and can be played on most DVD players and computer drives. This ensures that the musician can easily access and play their music on various devices, making it a convenient storage medium for sharing or playback purposes.

(d) (i) A secretary at Mambo Company, carried out the following task in the office on a particular day:

  • Making a personal emergency phone call using the company phone;
  • Opening and replying to office emails;
  • Typing the boss son's school project;
  • Typing a dismissal letter for an employee;
  • Planning the boss' diary;
  • Searching online for the latest office wear.

Categorize each task as either ethical or unethical. (3 marks)

Sample Answer:

  • Making a personal emergency phone call using the company phone: Ethical (in case of emergency)
  • Opening and replying to office emails: Ethical
  • Typing the boss son's school project: Unethical
  • Typing a dismissal letter for an employee: Ethical (part of job responsibilities)
  • Planning the boss' diary: Ethical
  • Searching online for the latest office wear: Unethical (personal use during work hours)

(ii) Daztech Company intends to secure data against loss. Outline three approaches they could use to achieve this. (3 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Regular data backups: Implementing a regular data backup schedule, whether to local storage, network-attached storage (NAS), or cloud services, ensures that data can be recovered in case of hardware failure, software corruption, or accidental deletion.
  2. Implementing data redundancy: Using RAID (Redundant Array of Independent Disks) configurations or other data redundancy techniques can protect against data loss due to hard drive failures. Redundancy ensures that if one drive fails, data is still accessible from other drives.
  3. Utilizing cloud storage: Storing critical data in the cloud provides offsite backup and disaster recovery capabilities. Cloud storage services offer data replication and geographically distributed data centers, protecting against local disasters and ensuring data availability.

4.

(a) Explain each of the following types of system software:

(i) Firmware; (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

Firmware is a type of system software that is embedded directly into hardware devices. It provides low-level control for the device's specific hardware. Firmware is typically stored in non-volatile memory (like ROM or flash memory) and is essential for the basic operation of hardware components, initializing them and providing instructions for other software to interact with the hardware.

(ii) Language translator. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

A language translator is system software that converts programming code from one language to another. In the context of computers, it typically refers to software that translates high-level programming languages (like C++, Java, Python) into machine code (binary instructions) that the computer's processor can understand and execute. Examples include compilers, interpreters, and assemblers.

(b) John was advised to patent his information communication technology innovation. Explain three reasons for this advice. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Protection from infringement: A patent grants John exclusive rights to his ICT innovation for a specified period. This legal protection prevents others from making, using, selling, or importing his invention without permission, safeguarding his intellectual property rights.
  2. Potential for commercialization and revenue generation: Patenting allows John to commercialize his innovation by licensing it to others or using it exclusively in his own products or services. This can generate revenue through royalties, licensing fees, or increased market share, turning his innovation into a profitable asset.
  3. Enhanced market position and competitive advantage: A patent can give John a significant competitive advantage in the market. It differentiates his innovation from competitors, enhances his company's reputation as an innovator, and can attract investors and partners, strengthening his market position.

5.

(a) Outline four characteristics of a twisted pair cable. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Twisted wires: Twisted pair cables consist of pairs of wires twisted together. This twisting helps to reduce electromagnetic interference (EMI) and crosstalk between the pairs, improving signal quality.
  2. Color-coded pairs: Wires are color-coded for easy identification and proper termination. Standard color codes ensure consistent wiring and help in troubleshooting network connections.
  3. Various categories: Twisted pair cables come in different categories (e.g., Cat5e, Cat6, Cat6a), each supporting different bandwidths and data transfer speeds. Higher categories offer better performance for faster networks.
  4. Shielded and unshielded options: Twisted pair cables are available in unshielded twisted pair (UTP) and shielded twisted pair (STP) versions. STP cables have an additional foil or braided shield around the wires to provide better protection against EMI, suitable for environments with high electrical noise.

(b)

(i) Explain the term emotions as used in life skills. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

In life skills, emotions refer to the feelings that individuals experience in response to situations, people, and events. Emotions are complex psychological states that involve physiological changes, subjective feelings, and associated behaviors. They play a crucial role in how people perceive the world, make decisions, and interact with others.

(ii) Describe two people skills that a student should possess. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Effective communication: This involves the ability to clearly and respectfully express ideas, listen actively to others, and understand both verbal and non-verbal cues. Good communication skills are essential for collaboration, building relationships, and resolving conflicts.
  2. Empathy: Empathy is the ability to understand and share the feelings of others. It allows students to connect with peers, teachers, and others on a deeper level, fostering positive relationships and creating a supportive learning environment. Empathy helps in teamwork, conflict resolution, and building strong interpersonal connections.

(c) Differentiate between privacy and confidentiality as used in ICT. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

While both privacy and confidentiality relate to data protection in ICT, they have distinct meanings:

  • Privacy: Refers to an individual's right to control their personal information. It's about deciding what information to share, with whom, and for what purpose. Privacy concerns the collection, use, and disclosure of personal data and the individual's autonomy over this information.
  • Confidentiality: Refers to the obligation to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access, disclosure, or use. It's about how entrusted information is handled and secured once it has been shared. Confidentiality focuses on preventing breaches of trust and maintaining the security of data that has been disclosed for a specific purpose.

(d) Vintech Company installed a laser printer for use. Explain three benefits the company would accrue from this printer. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. High print speed: Laser printers are known for their fast printing speeds, especially for large volumes of documents. This can significantly increase productivity in a busy office environment, allowing for quicker turnaround times for printing tasks.
  2. High print quality: Laser printers produce sharp, crisp text and images with high resolution. The print quality is consistent and professional, making them suitable for documents requiring a polished appearance, such as reports, presentations, and marketing materials.
  3. Cost-effectiveness in the long run: Although laser printers may have a higher initial cost than inkjet printers, they are often more cost-effective in the long run, especially for high-volume printing. Toner cartridges typically last longer and have a higher page yield than ink cartridges, reducing the cost per page and minimizing the frequency of replacements.

6.

(a)

(i) State three computers classified according to their functionality. (3 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Servers: Servers are computers designed to provide services to other computers (clients) over a network. They manage network resources, host websites, store files, and handle email, among other functions.
  2. Workstations: Workstations are high-performance computers intended for professional or technical applications, such as graphic design, video editing, and engineering. They offer more processing power, memory, and advanced graphics capabilities than typical desktop computers.
  3. Embedded computers: Embedded computers are specialized computers integrated into larger systems or devices to control their operations. They are found in a wide range of applications, from appliances and vehicles to industrial machinery and medical devices.

(ii) James installed the latest application software in his computer. Outline two features of this software. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. User-friendly interface: Latest application software often features an intuitive and user-friendly graphical user interface (GUI). This makes it easier for users to navigate the software, access features, and perform tasks efficiently, even without extensive technical knowledge.
  2. Enhanced security features: Modern application software typically includes advanced security features to protect user data and systems from threats. This can include features like built-in firewalls, encryption, malware detection, and regular security updates to address vulnerabilities and ensure data integrity and confidentiality.

(b) Differentiate between reliability and efficiency as used in computer software. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Reliability and efficiency are both important qualities of computer software, but they refer to different aspects:

  • Reliability: Refers to the ability of software to perform its intended functions correctly and consistently over a specified period. Reliable software operates without failures or errors under given conditions. Key aspects of reliability include stability, robustness (handling unexpected inputs or conditions), and fault tolerance (recovering from errors gracefully).
  • Efficiency: Refers to how effectively software utilizes system resources (such as CPU, memory, and storage) to perform its functions. Efficient software accomplishes tasks with minimal resource consumption and optimal performance. Key aspects of efficiency include speed, responsiveness, and resource utilization (e.g., memory footprint, CPU cycles).

(c) Figure 1 shows a computer input device. Use it to answer the questions that follow.

(i) Identify this device in Figure 1. (1 mark)

Sample Answer:

Touchpad.

(ii) Explain two disadvantage of the device identified in (i) (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Limited precision for detailed tasks: Touchpads can be less precise than mice for tasks requiring fine motor control, such as graphic design or detailed drawing. Navigating small interface elements or performing pixel-perfect selections can be challenging with a touchpad.
  2. Accidental activation: Touchpads can sometimes be accidentally activated by stray touches from palms or fingers while typing. This can lead to unintended cursor movements or clicks, disrupting workflow and requiring users to be mindful of hand placement to avoid accidental input.

(d) Explain three circumstances that could lead an organisation to develop a data protection policy document. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Increasing data breaches and cyber threats: As cyber threats and data breaches become more frequent and sophisticated, organizations need to establish formal policies to protect sensitive data from unauthorized access, loss, or theft. A data protection policy helps define security measures and incident response procedures.
  2. Regulatory compliance requirements: Data protection laws and regulations, such as GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) or CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act), mandate that organizations implement policies and procedures to safeguard personal data. Non-compliance can result in significant fines and reputational damage, making a data protection policy essential for legal compliance.
  3. Growing customer and stakeholder expectations: Customers and stakeholders increasingly expect organizations to handle their personal data responsibly and securely. A transparent and comprehensive data protection policy demonstrates an organization's commitment to data privacy, builds trust, and enhances its reputation, which is crucial for maintaining customer loyalty and stakeholder confidence.

7.

(a) Outline four ergonomic practices while working with a computer. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Proper posture: Maintain good posture by sitting upright with your back supported, shoulders relaxed, and feet flat on the floor or on a footrest. This helps prevent strain on your back, neck, and shoulders.
  2. Correct monitor placement: Position the monitor at arm's length and ensure the top of the screen is at or slightly below eye level. This reduces neck strain and eye fatigue.
  3. Adjustable chair: Use an adjustable chair that provides lumbar support, adjustable height, and armrests. Adjust the chair to fit your body and workstation setup to promote comfortable and healthy sitting.
  4. Regular breaks: Take frequent short breaks (e.g., every 20-30 minutes) to stand up, stretch, and move around. This helps prevent muscle fatigue, eye strain, and promotes better circulation.

(b) Explain each of the following terms as used in conflict management:

(i) smoothing; (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

Smoothing, in conflict management, is a strategy that emphasizes areas of agreement and downplays or ignores areas of disagreement. It aims to minimize conflict by focusing on maintaining harmony and good relationships, often by accommodating the other party's concerns while de-emphasizing one's own.

(ii) competing. (2 marks)

Sample Answer:

Competing is a conflict management style characterized by a high concern for one's own goals and a low concern for the other party's goals. It involves asserting one's position forcefully, often at the expense of the other party. This style is assertive and can be used when quick, decisive action is needed, but it may damage relationships if overused.

8.

(a) Outline four disadvantages of a flash disk as a computer storage device. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Limited lifespan due to write cycles: Flash disks have a limited number of write cycles, meaning they can only be written to and erased a finite number of times before their performance degrades or they fail completely. This limits their long-term durability, especially with frequent use for writing and erasing data.
  2. Potential for physical damage and loss: Flash disks are small and portable, making them susceptible to physical damage, loss, or theft. They can be easily misplaced, dropped, or damaged, leading to data loss if backups are not maintained.
  3. Security risks: Flash disks can be easily infected with viruses or malware if used on infected computers. They can also be lost or stolen, potentially exposing sensitive data if not properly encrypted or protected.
  4. Slower write speeds compared to SSDs: While read speeds of flash disks are generally fast, write speeds can be slower compared to Solid State Drives (SSDs), especially for larger files. This can make them less efficient for tasks involving frequent or large data writes.

(b) Software piracy is a highly practiced unethical behaviour in ICT. Explain three risks of this software to the user. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Security vulnerabilities: Pirated software often comes from unverified sources and may be bundled with malware, viruses, spyware, or other malicious software. Using pirated software exposes users to significant security risks, potentially leading to data breaches, identity theft, and system damage.
  2. Lack of updates and support: Pirated software typically does not receive updates or technical support from the original software vendor. This means users miss out on critical security patches, bug fixes, and new features, making the software less reliable and more vulnerable to threats over time.
  3. Legal consequences: Using pirated software is illegal and can result in legal penalties, including fines and lawsuits for copyright infringement. Organizations and individuals caught using pirated software can face significant financial and reputational damage.

(c) Mary was advised to be moving occasionally while using a computer. Explain three benefits of this movement. (6 marks)

Sample Answer:

  1. Reduced risk of musculoskeletal disorders: Prolonged static posture while using a computer can lead to musculoskeletal disorders like back pain, neck pain, and carpal tunnel syndrome. Occasional movement and stretching help to relieve muscle tension, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of developing these conditions.
  2. Improved blood circulation: Sitting for extended periods can restrict blood flow, especially to the legs and lower body. Regular movement, even simple stretches or walking around, helps to improve blood circulation, preventing stiffness and fatigue and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
  3. Increased energy levels and focus: Taking short breaks to move around can help combat fatigue and increase energy levels. Physical activity boosts alertness and improves cognitive function, leading to better concentration and productivity when returning to computer work.

(d) Distinguish between serial and parallel computer ports. (4 marks)

Sample Answer:

Serial and parallel ports are methods for data communication, differing in how data bits are transmitted:

  • Serial Port: Transmits data bits sequentially, one bit at a time, over a single wire or pair of wires. Serial communication is simpler and generally used for longer distances. Examples include USB and serial communication ports (RS-232).
  • Parallel Port: Transmits multiple data bits simultaneously over multiple parallel wires. Parallel communication is faster for short distances but more complex and expensive due to the need for multiple wires. An example is the older parallel printer port.

This paper consists of 4 printed pages.

Candidates should check the question paper to ascertain that all the pages are printed as indicated and that no questions are missing.

©2022 The Kenya National Examinations Council. Turn over

Exam code/number: 2920/101
Exam body name: THE KENYA NATIONAL EXAMINATIONS COUNCIL

INTRODUCTION TO INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY AND ETHICS

Subject name/Module title: INTRODUCTION TO INFORMATION COMMUNICATION TECHNOLOGY AND ETHICS

Exam duration: 3 hours
Exam date/period: November 2021
Module number: MODULE I

INSTRUCTIONS TO CANDIDATES

This paper consists of EIGHT questions.

Answer any FIVE of the EIGHT questions in the answer booklet provided.

Candidates should answer the questions in English.

1.

(a) Outline four advantages of the outsourcing method of acquiring computer services. (4 marks)
  1. Cost Savings: Outsourcing can reduce costs related to infrastructure, personnel, and technology.
  2. Focus on Core Competencies: It allows companies to concentrate on their main business activities by delegating IT functions to specialists.
  3. Access to Expertise: Outsourcing provides access to specialized skills and technologies that may not be available in-house.
  4. Increased Efficiency: Specialized outsourcing vendors can often deliver services more efficiently due to their focus and economies of scale.
(b) Describe three benefits of flash disks as storage devices. (6 marks)
  1. Portability: Flash disks are small and lightweight, making them highly portable and easy to carry around for data transfer between computers.
  2. Durability: They are more durable than traditional hard drives as they have no moving parts, making them resistant to shocks and physical damage.
  3. Fast Data Transfer Speeds: Flash disks, especially USB 3.0 and above, offer fast read and write speeds, enabling quick transfer of large files.
(c) Distinguish between defragmenter and debugger as used in computers. (4 marks)

A defragmenter is a utility that reorganizes fragmented files on a hard drive to improve access speed and system performance. It arranges file pieces contiguously. On the other hand, a debugger is a programming tool used to test and debug software programs. It helps programmers identify and fix errors (bugs) in the code by allowing them to step through the program execution, inspect variables, and set breakpoints.

(d) Sofia, a secretary at a technical institute, has low self-esteem. Explain three ways she could deal with this. (6 marks)
  1. Seek Professional Counseling: Therapy can help Sofia understand the root causes of her low self-esteem and develop coping mechanisms and strategies to build confidence.
  2. Focus on Strengths and Achievements: Sofia should make a conscious effort to recognize her accomplishments and skills. Keeping a journal of successes, no matter how small, can boost her self-perception.
  3. Practice Self-Compassion and Positive Self-Talk: Sofia should treat herself with kindness and understanding, especially during setbacks. Replacing negative self-talk with positive affirmations can gradually improve her self-image.

2.

(a) Outline three positive effects of Information Communication Technology to a college student. (3 marks)
  1. Enhanced Learning Resources: ICT provides access to a vast amount of online educational materials, e-libraries, and digital learning platforms, expanding learning opportunities beyond traditional textbooks.
  2. Improved Communication and Collaboration: ICT tools facilitate better communication with lecturers and peers through email, online forums, and collaborative platforms, enhancing group projects and discussions.
  3. Development of Digital Literacy Skills: Regular use of ICT in education equips students with essential digital skills that are highly valuable in the modern job market and for lifelong learning.
(b) Figure 1 shows a computer input device. Use it to answer the questions that follow.

(i) Identify the device shown in the Figure 1. (1 mark)

Mouse

(ii) Explain two advantages of the device identified in (i). (4 marks)
  1. User-Friendly Interface: A mouse provides a graphical and intuitive way to interact with a computer, making it easier for users to navigate and control applications compared to command-line interfaces.
  2. Increased Efficiency: Using a mouse for tasks like selecting text, dragging and dropping files, and navigating menus is generally faster and more efficient than using keyboard shortcuts alone.
(c) Explain three possible reasons that lead to unethical behaviour in Information Communication Technology. (6 marks)
  1. Lack of Awareness and Training: Insufficient understanding of ethical principles and guidelines in ICT can lead to unintentional unethical behavior. Lack of proper training on data privacy, intellectual property, and cyber security contributes to this.
  2. Pressure to Perform and Meet Targets: In competitive environments, individuals may feel pressured to compromise ethical standards to achieve targets, such as cutting corners in software development or data handling to meet deadlines.
  3. Ease of Anonymity and Reduced Accountability: The digital environment can provide anonymity, making it easier for individuals to engage in unethical activities without fear of immediate consequences or detection. This reduced accountability can encourage unethical actions.
(d) A programmer is developing a computer program for an organisation using a high-level language. Describe three characteristics of such languages. (6 marks)
  1. Readability and Understandability: High-level languages are designed to be closer to human language, making them easier to read, write, and understand compared to low-level languages. This improves programmer productivity.
  2. Machine Independence: Programs written in high-level languages are generally portable across different computer architectures. They are not tied to a specific hardware, as they need to be compiled or interpreted into machine code for each specific system.
  3. Abstraction: High-level languages provide a higher level of abstraction, hiding the complex details of computer hardware and machine code. This allows programmers to focus on the problem-solving logic rather than low-level implementation details.

3.

(a) Outline four advantages of a mesh computer network topology. (4 marks)
  1. High Reliability and Fault Tolerance: In a mesh topology, multiple paths exist between nodes. If one path fails, data can be rerouted through another path, ensuring network continuity.
  2. Enhanced Privacy and Security: Data can be transmitted through various paths, making it harder to intercept, thus potentially increasing security and privacy.
  3. Decentralized Communication: Mesh networks don't rely on a central node. This decentralization reduces the risk of a single point of failure bringing down the entire network.
  4. Scalability: It's relatively easy to add new nodes to a mesh network without disrupting the existing network infrastructure, making it scalable.
(b) Differentiate between minicomputer and microcomputer. (4 marks)

A minicomputer is a mid-range computer that is more powerful and capable than a microcomputer but less powerful than a mainframe computer. Minicomputers typically support multiple users simultaneously and are used in small to medium-sized organizations for tasks like server applications and data processing. A microcomputer, also known as a personal computer (PC), is a small, relatively inexpensive computer designed for individual use. Microcomputers are used for a wide range of applications, including word processing, internet browsing, and entertainment.

(c) George has noticed that his computer has been infected by a virus. Explain three symptoms this computer could have exhibited. (6 marks)
  1. Slow Performance: A virus can consume system resources, leading to a noticeable slowdown in computer speed, applications taking longer to load, and overall sluggishness.
  2. Frequent Crashes and Freezes: Viruses can cause system instability, resulting in frequent computer crashes, blue screens of death, and applications freezing or becoming unresponsive.
  3. Unusual Error Messages or Pop-ups: A virus might display strange error messages, unusual pop-up windows, or alerts that are not typical for the operating system or installed software.
(d) Plains Technical Institute computers have been attacked through the internet. Explain three cyber-crimes that could have been committed. (6 marks)
  1. Hacking (Unauthorized Access): Cybercriminals could have gained unauthorized access to the institute's computer systems to steal sensitive data, modify system settings, or disrupt operations.
  2. Malware Attack (Virus/Ransomware): The attack could involve the introduction of malware such as viruses or ransomware, which could corrupt or encrypt data, causing data loss or demanding a ransom for data recovery.
  3. Denial-of-Service (DoS) Attack: Attackers might have launched a DoS attack to overwhelm the institute's network resources, making their online services and internet access unavailable to legitimate users.

4.

(a) Outline four types of Read Only Memory (ROM). (4 marks)
  1. Mask ROM (MROM): Data is physically encoded into the circuit at the time of manufacture. It's the oldest type of ROM and cannot be altered after production.
  2. Programmable ROM (PROM): PROM can be programmed once by the user. It is initially blank, and users can write data using a special device called a PROM programmer. Once programmed, it cannot be erased or reprogrammed.
  3. Erasable Programmable ROM (EPROM): EPROM can be erased and reprogrammed, but the process is relatively slow and requires exposure to ultraviolet (UV) light to erase the data before reprogramming.
  4. Electrically Erasable Programmable ROM (EEPROM): EEPROM can be erased and reprogrammed electrically, without needing to be removed from the circuit. It allows byte-level erasure and reprogramming, making it more flexible than EPROM. Flash memory is a type of EEPROM.
(b) Distinguish between compromising and collaborating conflict management techniques. (4 marks)

Compromising is a conflict management technique where both parties involved in a conflict give up something to reach a mutually acceptable solution. It aims for a "middle ground" where neither party fully gets what they want, but both gain something. Collaborating is a conflict management technique where parties work together to find a solution that fully satisfies the concerns of both. It seeks a "win-win" outcome by integrating ideas and finding a creative solution that addresses everyone's needs.

(c) Delight Company intends to hire a network administrator. Explain three skills that this computer personnel should possess. (6 marks)
  1. Networking Knowledge: A network administrator must have a strong understanding of networking concepts, protocols (like TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP), network devices (routers, switches, firewalls), and network topologies to effectively manage and troubleshoot network infrastructure.
  2. Operating System Proficiency: They should be proficient in managing server operating systems (like Windows Server, Linux) and client operating systems. This includes user administration, system configuration, security settings, and performance monitoring.
  3. Problem-Solving and Troubleshooting Skills: Network administrators need excellent analytical and problem-solving skills to diagnose and resolve network issues quickly. This includes identifying network bottlenecks, troubleshooting connectivity problems, and restoring network services efficiently.
(d) Explain three circumstances under which a company could use telecommuting. (6 marks)
  1. Cost Reduction: Companies can reduce operational costs by allowing employees to telecommute. This can lead to savings on office space, utilities, and other office-related expenses.
  2. Improved Employee Morale and Productivity: Telecommuting can enhance employee job satisfaction and work-life balance, leading to increased morale and potentially higher productivity due to reduced commute stress and a more comfortable work environment.
  3. Access to a Wider Talent Pool: Telecommuting enables companies to hire talent from a broader geographical area, as physical location becomes less of a constraint. This allows access to specialized skills and expertise that may not be available locally.

5.

(a) Outline four ways a student could manage stress. (4 marks)
  1. Time Management and Organization: Planning and organizing study schedules, breaking down large tasks into smaller manageable parts, and prioritizing activities can reduce feelings of being overwhelmed and improve time management skills.
  2. Regular Exercise and Healthy Diet: Physical activity and a balanced diet are crucial for stress management. Exercise releases endorphins, which have mood-boosting effects, and proper nutrition supports overall well-being.
  3. Sufficient Sleep: Getting enough sleep is essential for both physical and mental health. Sleep deprivation can exacerbate stress, so maintaining a regular sleep schedule is important.
  4. Relaxation Techniques: Practicing relaxation techniques such as meditation, deep breathing exercises, or mindfulness can help calm the mind and reduce stress levels.
(b) Describe each of the following terms as used in Information Communication Technology:
(i) Information privacy; (2 marks)

Information privacy refers to the right of individuals to control how their personal information is collected, used, and disclosed. It encompasses the ability to determine what personal data is shared, with whom, and for what purpose, ensuring confidentiality and protection from unauthorized access or misuse.

(ii) Copyright; (2 marks)

Copyright is a legal right granted to the creator of original works of authorship, including literary, artistic, musical, and software works. It provides exclusive rights to control the reproduction, distribution, adaptation, public display, and performance of their work, protecting their intellectual property from unauthorized use.

(iii) Data protection. (2 marks)

Data protection involves a set of rules, policies, and procedures aimed at safeguarding personal data from unauthorized access, processing, loss, or damage. It includes measures to ensure data confidentiality, integrity, and availability, complying with legal and regulatory frameworks to protect individuals' privacy rights.

(c) Distinguish between dumb and intelligent terminals. (4 marks)

A dumb terminal is a computer terminal that relies entirely on the host computer for processing. It has minimal processing capabilities of its own and primarily serves as an input/output device. An intelligent terminal, also known as a smart terminal or thin client, has its own processing capabilities. While it can still connect to a host computer or server, it can perform some processing tasks locally, reducing the load on the central server and improving response times.

(d) Jucktech Technical Institute has been undertaking unsuccessful social responsibility programmes. Explain three possible causes of this failure. (6 marks)
  1. Poor Planning and Lack of Clear Objectives: Social responsibility programmes may fail if they are not properly planned with clear, measurable objectives and realistic timelines. Vague goals and inadequate preparation can lead to ineffective implementation and lack of impact.
  2. Insufficient Stakeholder Engagement: Failure to involve and engage with relevant stakeholders, including the community, beneficiaries, and employees, can result in programmes that do not address actual needs or lack community support and participation.
  3. Inadequate Resources and Funding: Social responsibility initiatives require sufficient resources, including financial, human, and material resources. Underfunding or lack of dedicated resources can hinder the effective execution and sustainability of these programmes.

6.

(a) Outline four benefits that a student could accrue from good time management. (4 marks)
  1. Reduced Stress: Effective time management helps students feel more in control of their workload, reducing stress and anxiety associated with deadlines and academic pressure.
  2. Improved Academic Performance: By managing time effectively, students can allocate sufficient time for studying, assignments, and revision, leading to better understanding of concepts and improved grades.
  3. Increased Productivity: Good time management enables students to accomplish more in less time by focusing on important tasks, minimizing procrastination, and utilizing study time efficiently.
  4. Better Work-Life Balance: Time management allows students to balance academic responsibilities with personal life, social activities, and hobbies, promoting overall well-being and preventing burnout.
(b) Amani Counselling Clinic has been handling many domestic conflict issues among its workers. Explain three possible causes of such conflicts. (6 marks)
  1. Work-Life Imbalance: Employees struggling to balance work responsibilities with family and personal life may experience stress and conflict at home, which can spill over into domestic conflicts.
  2. Financial Stress: Financial difficulties, such as job insecurity, wage stagnation, or unexpected expenses, can create tension and conflict within families, contributing to domestic disputes.
  3. Communication Breakdown: Poor communication patterns, misunderstandings, and lack of effective communication skills within families can escalate minor disagreements into larger conflicts and domestic issues.
(c) Figure 2 shows computer ports. Use it to answer the questions that follow.

(i) Identify each of the ports depicted in the figure; (2 marks)

(I) USB Port

(II) Ethernet Port (RJ45)

(ii) Explain one use of each of the ports identified in (i). (4 marks)

USB Port: Used to connect a wide range of peripheral devices to a computer, such as keyboards, mice, printers, external hard drives, and flash drives for data transfer and device charging.

Ethernet Port (RJ45): Used to connect a computer to a network (LAN) via an Ethernet cable for wired internet access and network communication, enabling file sharing and network printing.

(d) Julius installed an application software in his computer. Describe two classes of this software that he could have installed. (4 marks)
  1. Productivity Software: This class includes applications designed to enhance productivity, such as word processors (e.g., Microsoft Word), spreadsheets (e.g., Microsoft Excel), presentation software (e.g., Microsoft PowerPoint), and database management systems (e.g., Microsoft Access). These tools help users create documents, manage data, and perform various office tasks efficiently.
  2. Utility Software: Utility software is designed to support and optimize the computer's infrastructure and system performance. Examples include antivirus software, disk defragmentation tools, system monitoring tools, file compression utilities (e.g., WinZip), and backup software. These applications help maintain system security, improve performance, and manage computer resources.

7.

(a) Outline four types of people skills that should be possessed by an Information Communication Technology graduate. (4 marks)
  1. Communication Skills: ICT professionals need strong verbal and written communication skills to effectively explain technical concepts to non-technical users, collaborate with team members, and document processes and procedures.
  2. Teamwork and Collaboration: ICT projects often involve working in teams. Graduates should be able to collaborate effectively, share knowledge, and contribute to team goals in diverse and multidisciplinary environments.
  3. Problem-Solving Skills: People skills in problem-solving involve the ability to understand user needs, analyze issues from different perspectives, and work collaboratively to find effective solutions that meet both technical and user requirements.
  4. Customer Service Orientation: Many ICT roles involve direct interaction with users or clients. People skills related to customer service include patience, empathy, active listening, and the ability to provide support and resolve user issues in a friendly and helpful manner.
(b) Explain three circumstances that could necessitate the use of a batch method in data processing. (6 marks)
  1. Large Volume of Data: When processing a very large amount of data that accumulates over time, batch processing is efficient. It allows for processing data in bulk during off-peak hours, reducing system load during peak times.
  2. Non-Time-Critical Processing: For tasks where immediate results are not required and there is no need for real-time interaction, batch processing is suitable. Examples include payroll processing, end-of-month financial reports, and generating bulk mailing lists.
  3. Cost-Effectiveness: Batch processing can be more cost-effective for certain tasks as it can be scheduled to run during off-peak hours when computing resources may be cheaper or more readily available. It also reduces the need for continuous system monitoring and immediate response systems.
(c) Differentiate between logical and physical access controls as used in data security. (4 marks)

Logical access controls are security measures that control access to computer systems, networks, data, and applications. They are software-based and include measures like passwords, usernames, access control lists (ACLs), encryption, and authentication protocols. Physical access controls are security measures that restrict physical access to facilities and resources, such as data centers, server rooms, and offices. They include measures like security guards, locks, biometric scanners, security badges, and surveillance cameras.

(d) Jacob, an ICT manager in a company, advised the management to acquire a mainframe computer for networking. Explain three reasons that could have led to this advice. (6 marks)
  1. High Processing Power and Performance: Mainframe computers are designed to handle extremely large volumes of data and transactions with high speed and reliability. For a company with extensive data processing needs and a large number of users, a mainframe can provide the necessary processing power to ensure smooth and efficient operations.
  2. Scalability and Reliability: Mainframes are highly scalable, allowing them to handle increasing workloads and user demands as the company grows. They are also renowned for their exceptional reliability and uptime, crucial for businesses that require continuous operation and minimal downtime for critical applications.
  3. Enhanced Security and Centralized Management: Mainframes offer robust security features and centralized management capabilities. They provide a secure environment for sensitive data and enable centralized control over system resources, user access, and security policies, simplifying administration and enhancing overall security posture.

8.

(a) Explain three characteristics of real-time data processing method. (6 marks)
  1. Immediate Processing: Real-time processing requires data to be processed immediately as it is generated or received, with minimal delay. This is crucial for applications where timely responses are critical, such as air traffic control systems or online transaction processing.
  2. Continuous Data Flow: Real-time systems typically handle a continuous stream of input data and produce outputs in a continuous and ongoing manner. They are designed to operate without interruption and maintain a constant flow of data processing.
  3. High Speed and Responsiveness: Real-time processing systems are characterized by their high speed and responsiveness. They must be able to process data and generate results quickly enough to meet strict time constraints and ensure timely responses in dynamic environments.
(b) Explain two circumstances that could lead to upgrading of a computer hard disk. (4 marks)
  1. Insufficient Storage Capacity: When the existing hard disk is consistently running out of storage space due to increasing data volume, software installations, or multimedia files, an upgrade to a larger capacity hard disk becomes necessary to accommodate the growing storage needs.
  2. Performance Degradation: If the computer's performance is slowing down due to a slow or failing hard disk, upgrading to a faster hard disk, such as an SSD (Solid State Drive), can significantly improve data access speeds, boot times, and overall system responsiveness.
(c) An ICT technician prefers to use a fibre optic cable in a computer network. Explain three reasons for this preference. (6 marks)
  1. Higher Bandwidth and Speed: Fibre optic cables offer significantly higher bandwidth and data transfer speeds compared to copper cables. They can transmit data at much faster rates, making them ideal for high-speed network applications and large data transfers.
  2. Greater Distance and Signal Integrity: Fibre optic cables can transmit data over much longer distances without signal degradation compared to copper cables. They maintain signal integrity over long distances, ensuring reliable data transmission even in geographically dispersed networks.
  3. Immunity to Electromagnetic Interference (EMI): Fibre optic cables are immune to electromagnetic interference and radio frequency interference (RFI) because they transmit data as light pulses rather than electrical signals. This makes them more reliable in environments with electrical noise and reduces the risk of data corruption.
(d) A company used ICT legislation and guidelines in developing its ICT policy. Outline four sources of these legislations and guidelines. (4 marks)
  1. National ICT Acts and Laws: Legislation passed by the national government related to information and communication technology, such as cybercrime laws, data protection acts, and electronic transaction laws, form a primary source of guidelines for ICT policies.
  2. International Standards and Frameworks: Global standards and frameworks like ISO 27001 for information security management, GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), and other international cybersecurity and privacy standards provide benchmarks and best practices for ICT policy development.
  3. Industry-Specific Regulations: Certain industries, such as healthcare, finance, and education, may have specific regulatory requirements and guidelines related to ICT and data handling. These industry-specific regulations must be considered in ICT policy formulation.
  4. Organizational Policies and Codes of Conduct: Internal organizational policies, ethical codes of conduct, and corporate governance guidelines also influence the development of ICT policies. These internal documents reflect the company's values, ethical standards, and operational principles related to technology use.

This paper consists of 4 printed pages.

© 2021 The Kenya National Examinations Council.

Turn over

THIS IS THE LAST PRINTED PAGE.
